metadata.title:
ADVANCED


GROUND ON
FRANCHISES

CHARTER COMMISSION AGREES
UNANIMOUSLY TO MANY


PROGRESSIVE IDEAS.

pages.raw_text:
A position in advance of the laws
Of the state of Washington is taken
in the draft of the proposed new
city charter on the question of
franchises, unanimously agreed
upon at the meeting of the charter
revision commission last night.


The possibility of the city acquir-
ing street railway, telephone. gas
or lighting plants hereafter to be
installed is granted, also the right
of the city to require space for its
wires in all underground conduits
and other improvements of this
kind. A common user clause has
been inserted permitting subse-
quent franchises to share in the
rights of those already granted,
and many other new and advanced
ideas are included.


The charter commission met last
night and concluded to have the
mayor elected by the five commits
sioners, instead of voting direct on
the mayor as at present. An effort
to have the position of mayor
designated in the election was
made by H. d. Merritt, Gordon G.
Corbaley, Warren w. Tolman, B. r.
Ostrander and John E. Blair, all
friends of Mayor Pratt, but it was
lost by vote of eight to five.


Nearly one-half of the charter
was disposed of last night, and the
rest will be considered at the meet
ing next Tuesday night. 1t now
begins to appear as if the commit
tee will get together in due time
and report a charter that will be
satisfactory to the people and the
most advanced step in municipal
government ever taken west of the
mountains.
